content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and K, denoting the number of elements in the array and the parameter mentioned in the problem statement. The next line contains N space-separated integers, denoting the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: The minimum number of elements that need to be inserted so that the infinite loop can be avoided.

sampleTestCases.explanation: You can win the game by inserting the element 2 into the array.
The initial array will result in termination in the first step of the algorithm only. Thus, there is no need to insert any new element.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to understand the conditions under which the infinite loop occurs. The loop occurs when the size of the array 'b' is not equal to 2^k. We can use bitwise operations to generate all possible subsets of the array 'a' and calculate their bitwise OR. We then check if the size of the array 'b' is equal to 2^k. If not, we need to insert the minimum number of elements to make the size of 'b' equal to 2^k.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * 2^N * K) - where T is the number of test cases, N is the maximum number of elements in the array, and K is the maximum value of K.
